titles.title: To require the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate Haiti for temporary protected status.
To require the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate Haiti for temporary protected status.
To require the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate Haiti for temporary protected status.

action.text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
Considered under the provisions of rule H. Res. 965.
DEBATE - The House proceeded with one hour of debate on H.R. 1689.
DEBATE - The House resumed debate on H.R. 1689.
The previous question was ordered pursuant to the rule.

vote.text: On passage Passed by the Yeas and Nays: 224 - 204 (Roll no. 120).

reference.label: text of amendment in the nature of a substitute

action.text: Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
Read the first time. Placed on Senate Legislative Calendar under Read the First Time.

calendar.text: Read the second time. Placed on Senate Legislative Calendar under General Orders. Calendar No. 374.

bill.summary: This bill directs the Department of Homeland Security to designate Haiti for temporary protected status (TPS) for 18 months beginning August 3, 2025. (Eligible nationals of a TPS-designated country may obtain employment authorization, may not be detained on the basis of their immigration status, and are not subject to removal while they retain TPS.)
